question_text:
Product of Array Except Self

Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i].

The product of any prefix or suffix of nums is guaranteed to fit in a 32-bit integer.

You must write an algorithm that runs in O(n) time and without using the division operation.
input_format: A single line of space-separated integers.
output_format: A single line of space-separated integers representing the product array.
constraints: The product of any prefix or suffix of nums is guaranteed to fit in a 32-bit integer.
explanation: We use prefix and suffix products. First pass (left-to-right): `prefix[i]` = product of all elements before index i. Second pass (right-to-left): multiply each prefix product by the running suffix product (product of all elements after index i). This avoids division and runs in O(N) time, O(N) space for the output array. The key insight: `answer[i] = product_of_all_left_of_i × product_of_all_right_of_i`, achievable without ever dividing by storing cumulative products from both directions.
